heading: Action for infringement of a patent containing an invalid claim
label: 288 Action for infringement of a patent containing an invalid claim
text: Whenever a claim of a patent is invalid, an action may be maintained for the infringement of a claim of the patent which may be valid. The patentee shall recover no costs unless a disclaimer of the invalid claim has been entered at the Patent and Trademark Office before the commencement of the suit.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on Title 35, U.S.C., 1946 ed., § 71 (R.S. 4922).The necessity for a disclaimer to recover on valid claims is eliminated. See section 253.Language is changed.
Amendments2011— struck out “, without deceptive intention,” after “Whenever”.Pub. L. 112–291975— substituted “Patent and Trademark Office” for “Patent Office”.Pub. L. 93–596
